2604.27924.title: Can AI Be a Good Peer Reviewer? A Survey of Peer Review Process, Evaluation, and the Future
2604.27924.paper_summary: Peer review is a multi-stage process involving reviews, rebuttals, meta-reviews, final decisions, and subsequent manuscript revisions. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have motivated methods that assist or automate different stages of this pipeline. In this survey, we synthesize techniques for (i) peer review generation, including fine-tuning strategies, agent-based systems, RL-based methods, and emerging paradigms to enhance generation; (ii) after-review tasks including rebuttals, meta-review and revision aligned to reviews; and (iii) evaluation methods spanning human-centered, reference-based, LLM-based and aspect-oriented. We catalog datasets, compare modeling choices, and discuss limitations, ethical concerns, and future directions. The survey aims to provide practical guidance for building, evaluating, and integrating LLM systems across the full peer review workflow.
2604.27924.paper_summary_zh: 同行評審是一個多階段的過程，涉及評審、反駁、元評審、最終決策以及隨後的手稿修訂。最近在大型語言模型（LLMs）方面的進展促使了幫助或自動化此流程不同階段的方法。在本次調查中，我們綜合了以下技術：（i）同行評審生成，包括微調策略、基於代理的系統、基於強化學習的方法以及增強生成的新興範式；（ii）評審後的任務，包括反駁、元評審和根據評審進行的修訂；以及（iii）涵蓋以人為中心、基於參考、基於LLM和面向方面的評估方法。我們編目數據集，比較建模選擇，並討論限制、倫理問題和未來方向。該調查旨在為構建、評估和整合LLM系統提供實用指導，涵蓋完整的同行評審工作流程。
2604.27924.author: Sihong Wu et.al.
2604.27924.authors: Sihong Wu, Owen Jiang, Yilun Zhao, Tiansheng Hu, Yiling Ma, Kaiyan Zhang, Manasi Patwardhan, Arman Cohan
